text-block-1.title: Building Blocks of the Interface
text-block-1.text:
Sling components are reusable content building blocks. Each component has a resource type that determines how it's rendered. Think of them as the visible manifestations of data in the Matrix - the code translated into perceivable reality.

Components are organized in /apps under their application name and can include scripts (HTL, JSP), dialogs, and clientlibs.

code-block-1.title: Component Definition
code-block-1.code: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <jcr:root xmlns:jcr="http://www.jcp.org/jcr/1.0" xmlns:sling="http://sling.apache.org/jcr/sling/1.0" jcr:primaryType="sling:Component" jcr:title="Matrix Code Block" jcr:description="Displays digital rain code" componentGroup="Sling Matrix" sling:resourceSuperType="sling-matrix/components/base"/>

text-block-2.title: Component Structure
text-block-2.text:
A typical Sling component directory contains:

  • .content.xml - Component metadata and properties
  • component.html - HTL rendering script
  • _cq_dialog/.content.xml - Author dialog definition
  • clientlibs/ - CSS and JavaScript assets
  • Java classes - Sling Models or WCMUsePojo classes
